description:
A customer-facing support script needs a tiny “footprint test” to prove that a live system
    can still reach a few essential diagnostic files.  
    You have an empty session on a freshly-booted Linux server whose prompt drops you
    inside the account “user” (home directory: /home/user).

    Goal
        1. Make sure there is a directory called /home/user/support  
           (create it if it is missing).
        2. Inside that directory create a plain text file named collected_files.txt.

    VERY STRICT content requirements for /home/user/support/collected_files.txt  
        • The file must contain exactly three text lines, each line terminated with a single
          UNIX newline character “\n”.  
        • No blank lines, no leading or trailing spaces.  
        • The required lines, in the required order, are:

              /etc/os-release
              /etc/hosts
              /proc/cpuinfo

    Constraint
        • Perform everything with ONE single compound shell command (one‐liner)
          that you type at the terminal — no editing in nano/vim, no multi-line
          here-documents, no calling external scripts.

    When you are done, you should be able to run:

        cat /home/user/support/collected_files.txt

    …and see exactly the three paths, one per line, with a newline after the last line.

    This is a simple check often used by support engineers before running larger data
    collectors, so follow the format to the letter; the automated test will compare the file
    byte-for-byte.
truth:
After the agent finishes successfully, the following must be true:

    1. Directory:
        Path             : /home/user/support
        Type             : directory
        Permissions      : default “mkdir” user umask (test does NOT verify permissions).

    2. File:
        Path             : /home/user/support/collected_files.txt
        Type             : text file (ASCII/UTF-8)
        Exact byte content (including the final trailing newline, \n shown literally):

            /etc/os-release\n
            /etc/hosts\n
            /proc/cpuinfo\n

        No additional characters, spaces or blank lines are allowed before, after or
        between these three lines.
name: task_000000_cd1cec07
